baseline_reuse_justification: Captured a fresh baseline this run to guarantee identical machine state with the candidate (M14 baseline 20260628T090101.820031Z is 24h old and pre-dates the M15 fallback commit 5151f89; the new baseline re-confirms engine HEAD behavior is unchanged on the non-DFlash path with 10/10 rows error-free and decode_tps in 22.9-31.3 range, very close to M14 baseline).

candidate_row_findings: candidate row 1: completion tokens 16 >= 1, but text repeats 'assistant\n\n<think>' suffix; keyword 'ok' hit but visible-thinking leak via 'thinking' / '</think>' substring
candidate row 2: same keyword hit, same visible-thinking leak
candidate row 1: repeated 5-gram count 6 exceeds 3 (loops on 'The answer is: The answer is:')
candidate row 2: repeated 5-gram count 4 exceeds 3 + repeated line ratio 0.571 >= 0.500 (loops on 'Paris is capital of France.user\n\nassistant')
candidate row 1: forbidden substrings 'Thinking Process' / 'thinking' found (visible-thinking leak)
candidate row 2: same forbidden substring leak
candidate row 1: missing prompt keywords: risk, mitigation, owner; invalid exact JSON object: Extra data; repeated line ratio 0.769 >= 0.500
candidate row 2: same missing keywords, same invalid JSON, same repeated line ratio

second_sample_skipped_reason: Lane description forbids additional heavyweight repeated samples when quality_compare status=fail on every prompt. No quality-passing sample exists to repeat against, so the >=2 quality-passing requirement cannot be satisfied regardless of the second sample's metric movement.
decision_rationale: quality_compare.py status=fail on all 5 deterministic prompts (m14_dflash_short_factual, m14_dflash_brief_summary, m14_dflash_code_function, m14_dflash_json_output, m14_dflash_math_calc).

Every prompt fails both output-quality thresholds (repeated 5-gram > 3, repeated line ratio >= 0.5, missing keywords, forbidden thinking substrings, invalid JSON) and timing thresholds (TTFT +57-720%, decode TPS -7-52%, total +17-788%).

Adaptive scheduling+fallback is stable (no row errors, every row carries fallback_status=fallback_pathological_target_only, target_verify=True preserved on every emission, sequential_text_only=true) but the drafter is invoked so rarely (draft_rounds 1-7 across 14-96 total rounds) that the per-emission target verify cost dominates the latency profile and the target_correction logic still introduces output-quality loops.
There is no metric in which the adaptive DFlash candidate produces a repeatable latency win over the non-DFlash baseline; every metric on every prompt regresses.

The M14 closeout (2026-06-28) already closed the prior fixed-block-size lanes as REJECT; the adaptive scheduling+fallback lane cannot close the per-emission target-verify overhead with this prompt mix, so the M15 quality/performance evaluation closes as REJECT as well.
DFlash remains default-off and KEEP OPT-IN per AGENTS.md and the M14 closeout rationale. No promotion lane opens from this evidence.

fulfills_assertion_statuses.VAL-M15-005: satisfied — quality_compare.py status recorded (status=fail); row-error inspection completed for both baseline and candidate reports (10/10 rows error-free); no JSON/math/repetition regression masked by the gate (every regression is surfaced by the gate).
fulfills_assertion_statuses.VAL-M15-006: satisfied — second repeated sample not collected (lane description forbids it after status=fail); decision REJECT recorded with evidence paths; explicit default-off rationale captured; DFlash stays opt-in.
